seoTitle: Print a Profit & Loss Report in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to generate, customize, and print a Profit & Loss report in QuickBooks Online to review revenue, expenses, and overall business performance.
intro: A Profit & Loss report gives you a clear snapshot of how your business is performing over a given period. This guide covers how to pull that report up in QuickBooks Online, tailor it to your needs, and print it for records or presentations.

steps.heading: Access the reports section
steps.body: Navigate to the Reports area of QuickBooks Online and locate the Profit & Loss report from the standard reports list.

steps.heading: Customize the report
steps.body: Adjust the date range, comparison periods, and level of detail so the report reflects exactly the timeframe and breakdown you need.

steps.heading: Review your financial performance
steps.body: Look over the totals for revenue and expenses to understand profitability trends before finalizing the report.

steps.heading: Print or share the report
steps.body: Use the print option to generate a hard copy, or export it as a PDF to share with stakeholders or your accountant.

faq.question: Can I customize which accounts appear on the report?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online lets you filter and group accounts on the Profit & Loss report to match how you want to present the data.

faq.question: How often should I generate a Profit & Loss report?
faq.answer: Many businesses review it monthly or quarterly, though you can run it for any custom date range as often as you need.

faq.question: Can I automate regular Profit & Loss reporting?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online supports scheduling and automation features that can send recurring reports, cutting down on manual work.

faq.question: Does printing the report affect my saved report settings?
faq.answer: No, printing just outputs the current view; your customized report settings remain saved for future use.
